disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Cannot lie flat; needs three pillows and wakes suddenly gasping for air at night
trigger_question_examples: Can you lie down flat, or do you need to sit up to breathe?
Do you ever wake up in the night suddenly short of breath?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I can't lie flat at all — I have to sit straight up on three pillows, and last night I woke up gasping like I was drowning.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Progressive breathlessness on minimal exertion over two weeks, sharply worse over 48 hours
trigger_question_examples: How long has the breathlessness been building up?
What can you still do before you get puffed?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's been creeping up for a couple of weeks, but the last two days it's got much worse — even getting to the chair leaves me winded.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Rapidly progressive bilateral leg swelling spreading up to the thighs, with quick weight gain
trigger_question_examples: Have you noticed any swelling in your legs?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Both my legs have blown up and it's gone right up my thighs, and I've put on about 5 kilos really fast.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Regular diuretic ('water tablet') missed for the last three days due to a facility refill error
trigger_question_examples: Have you been able to take all your usual medicines?
Have you missed any doses of your water tablet?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Now that you mention it, the staff said I missed my water tablet for the last three days — there was a mix-up with the prescription refill.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Struggling to breathe at rest, using accessory muscles, with a low home oximeter reading of 91%
trigger_question_examples: How hard are you having to work to breathe right now?
Do you have an oxygen or finger clip reading?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm working really hard just to breathe sitting still — the staff's finger clip is showing 91.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever, no cough with coloured phlegm, no sudden one-sided chest pain (pertinent negatives)
trigger_question_examples: Any fevers, or a cough bringing up coloured phlegm?
Did any of this start with a sudden sharp chest pain?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fevers, and I'm not coughing up anything coloured. It didn't come on as a sudden sharp pain — it built up.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: High falls risk from heavy legs and frequent night-time trips to the toilet
trigger_question_examples: Are you steady on your feet at the moment?
How are you managing getting to the toilet?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My legs are so heavy I'm frightened I'll fall, especially getting up to the toilet at night.
